tweet: Our work is not over until that is true, until we eradicate the fundamental problems and prejudice that allows for these miscarriages of justice to happen in the first place. 12/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: It is well past time for the leaders of our country to act and make policy changes to combat discrimination against marginalized communities.  The color of your skin should not dictate your quality of education, housing, health care, or safety.  11/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: Law enforcement is a dangerous profession and although most police officers honor their oath to protect and serve their communities, we cannot continue to ignore the abuse of power and discrimination against people of color by those that do not.  10/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: I promise to continue to do more and do better, to listen and learn from Black and Brown community leaders and constituents I am honored to serve.  I will work together with members of our community, elected officials, and anyone who is willing to stand on the side of justice. 9/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: How can we end the disparities in housing, health care, education, and justice for communities of color?  How can we break down systemic racism once and for all?  These questions demand thoughtful answers with real solutions. We have the responsibility to act. 8/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: I’ve joined peaceful protests, spoken with community leaders, and engaged with law enforcement searching for answers to the questions:   How can we stop this from happening again and again?  How can we hold bad actors accountable?   7/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: The response should not be more violence as violence begets violence & perpetuates hate, division, & injustice.   It is my responsibility - and that of all our leaders - to listen & learn & commit to working together to bring accountability, justice, & ultimately, change.  6/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: George Floyd, Ahmaud Arbery, Breonna Taylor, and those before them deserve accountability and justice above all.  We must stay focused on finding solutions to the very real problems caused by racial discrimination, while also being respectful to our fellow Americans. 5/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: Unfortunately, in some places we’ve seen this pain co-opted by looters & people who seek to divide & distract us.  Violence & looting are not acts of political expression, they’re criminal. We cannot allow violence by few to take away from those who seek to make real change. 4/

name: Rep. Anthony Brindisi
tweet: In America, the right to peacefully protest is an inalienable one, which must be protected by all elected leaders no matter what. I hope the messages being shared in recent days by people of color about the effects of systemic & institutionalized racism will be met w/ action. 3/

tweet: That pain has poured out into the streets with peaceful demonstrations across the world and right here in our communities.   I support the countless Americans participating in these peaceful demonstrations. 2/
